trivia_bullets.text: Invert // Invent received a day-zero errata before GRN's release. The Invert face was printed without the words 'until end of turn,' which would have made the power/toughness switch permanent. Mark Rosewater stated: 'The card had that text for most of its existence. During one of the many back and forth between R&D and CAPS (the department who gets the cards printed), it accidentally got removed and it didn't get noticed.' The card is tagged day-zero-errata on Scryfall.
sources: epicstream.com: MTG Head Designer Explains How The Simple Guilds of Ravnica Mistake Slipped Through

trivia_bullets.text: Released in Guilds of Ravnica (October 5, 2018) with the Izzet watermark on both faces. As a split card, Invert costs a single hybrid {U/R} mana (playable as one blue or one red), while Invent costs {4}{U}{R} and tutors for an instant and/or sorcery — a classic Izzet toolbox package on one card.

Invert // Invent is not legal in Pauper despite both halves being instant-type spells at uncommon. Scryfall marks it not_legal in Pauper; split-card legality is governed by the higher combined mana value at time of printing. EDHREC rank 12,117; the Invent half's tutor effect is the primary Commander draw.
